# PSFTP Message Stream Golden Vectors

These vectors characterize deterministic PSFTP RFC60/RFC76 encoding before KMP migration. They cover request, query, notification, and RFC76 frame splitting rules that are shared between Android and iOS, and `PsFtpByteCodecCommonPolicyTest.kt` now consumes them as executable shared common policy coverage. Request streams with extra file data remain Android-specific in the current utility API and are pinned as an explicit byte-codec policy before higher-level fake-transport write tests choose facade behavior.

`byte-codec-readiness.json` names the pre-migration behavior-family gate for PSFTP byte-codec extraction across RFC76 frame and RFC60 message-stream vectors: `rfc76-header` next/status/sequence/payload decoding, RFC76 error-frame platform split, request/query/notification stream encoding, Android request file-data append behavior, MTU frame splitting, sequence wrap, platform codec vector references, and the `compile-verification-gate` must remain covered before production byte-codec logic moves into shared KMP.
